---
title: "rk4"
generated: true
description: "Integrate $dy/dt = f(t, y)$ with classic 4th-order Runge--Kutta."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.numerics.ode`](../nitrix-numerics-ode.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:rk4 -->
```python
rk4(f: VectorField, y0: Float[Array, '...'], t: Float[Array, 't']) -> Float[Array, 't ...']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:rk4 -->
Integrate $dy/dt = f(t, y)$ with classic 4th-order Runge--Kutta.

Same interface as [`euler`](../nitrix-numerics-ode/euler.qmd); far more accurate per step (4th-order
local error), the sensible default for smooth vector fields.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:rk4 -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `f` | `VectorField` | — | — | Vector field `f(t, y) -> dy/dt` (`t` scalar, `y` the state). |
| `y0` | `Float` | `...` | — | Initial state at `t[0]`. |
| `t` | `Float` | `t` | — | Strictly monotone time points of shape `(t,)`; one RK4 step is taken per interval. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `t` × `...`. States at each time point, shape `(len(t), *y0.shape)` (`ys[0]` is `y0`).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:rk4 -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `t` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
